target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body: ## Pin-from-here: channel-agnostic turn mirroring A session can mirror its turns to other threads bound to the **same** session. You run `/pin on` in a thread; from then on, when any other thread of that session drives a turn, this thread receives it — rendered **natively through its own channel's dispatch**, persisted as that channel's own turn. ### Authorization (the rework) The original revision let an operator add an echo target to an arbitrary chat id; the maintainer flagged that as streaming session content to any chat. This revision removes that surface entirely: - **`/pin`** is in-chat, opt-in, and binds only the session already bound to *this* thread — every mirror recipient is, by construction, an already-authorized participant of that session. There is no "stream to an arbitrary chat id" path. - Operator echo adds require a session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`), fail-closed. ### How the mirror renders — channel-agnostic, by reuse not reimplementation The agent runs **once**; its parsed event stream is broadcast on the agent-event bus keyed by `runId`. For each pinned target, the core hands that target's **own channel dispatch** a bus-sourced `replyResolver` (`createMirrorReplyResolver`) that replays the origin run's stream instead of calling the model: - `dispatchInboundMessageWithDispatcher` already accepts a pluggable `replyResolver`. `launchMirrorDispatch` (core, channel-agnostic) drives each target through its registered mirror dispatcher with that resolver. - Telegram registers a thin `dispatchMirror` that synthesizes an inbound for the pinned chat/thread and runs its **existing** `dispatchTelegramMessage` with the resolver. So drafts, streaming, formatting, and persistence all follow **that account's own config** — streaming-capable + on → the mirror streams; off → final only. Nothing is forced; nothing is reimplemented per channel. - **Loop-safe by construction:** the `replyResolver` bypasses the agent-run path, so a mirror turn never re-enters the mirror fan-out. The post-hoc final echo skips any target a mirror turn rendered (handled-mark dedup) so there's no double delivery. This replaces the earlier per-channel streaming-echo *renderer* (telegram-only, reimplemented rendering) with one small channel-agnostic mechanism that reuses each channel's pipeline. **Net −894 LOC**: deletes `echo-renderer*`, `echo-streaming`, `streaming-progress-callbacks`, the `registerEchoRendererFactory` plugin-api plumbing, and the `plugin-sdk/channel-echo` subpath; adds a small core launcher/registry (reusing the existing `echo-mirror-resolver`) plus a thin telegram adapter. ### Real behavior proof (Telegram-desktop, mantis-equivalent) Recreation of the Mantis telegram-desktop flow on a local patched crabbox: a real gateway built from this branch (current `openclaw/openclaw` main + this work), Telegram Desktop + the user-driver + screen capture, driven by a **real `claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6` backend with an exec tool call** (not a mock). A Telegram thread runs `/pin on`; webchat then drives the same session with a tool-using prompt (`sleep 3; hostname` — output not derivable from context). The pinned thread renders the **webchat-origin turn's reply natively** — including the real tool result — through telegram's own dispatch: 1. `/verbose on` → "⚙️ Verbose logging enabled." 2. `/pin on` → **"📌 Pinned. This thread will mirror turns from other threads of this session."** 3. `📱 [via webchat] Use your exec (bash) tool to run … sleep 3; hostname …` 4. The pinned thread receives the real reply: **"The hostname is `crabbox-golden-crayfish-fa445ed1`."** — the actual box hostname, i.e. a real `hostname` exec run by the webchat-triggered turn, rendered natively in the pinned Telegram thread via the channel-agnostic mirror dispatch. Mechanism confirmed in the gateway log for the filmed run: `launchMirrorDispatch` fires for the webchat-origin turn (origin = webchat, telegram target not self-excluded, dispatcher found); the telegram `dispatchMirror` builds the synthetic inbound (`context=BUILT`) and runs `dispatchTelegramMessage` for the full turn (`message processed: channel=telegram messageId=0 … completed`); exactly one reply lands (post-hoc assistant echo skipped by the handled-mark, no duplicate). ### Tests + CI - `tsgo` core + extensions: clean on the changed surface (pre-existing `copilot` and `ui` errors are upstream). `oxlint` on the changed files: clean. - Echo/mirror unit tests pass (`echo`, `echo-mirror-resolver`, `chat.send-user-echo`, registry); the bus-sourced resolver, handled-mark dedup, and `/pin` fan-out are covered. ### AI assistance Built with AI assistance (Claude). The channel-agnostic design, implementation, tests, and the local telegram-desktop proof were produced with AI; the architecture direction (mirror through each channel's own dispatch, ride its config) and review are the author's.
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia
commands.command: re-review — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. the operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetmatchessessionparticipant`). also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`originatingchannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror
commands.freeform_prompt: — reshaped the mirror surface to **pin-from-here** to address the arbitrary-chat-id / authz concern: a thread opts itself in with `/pin on` and can only mirror the session it is already bound to (no arbitrary-target argument), so a pin never crosses a session boundary and adds zero new authorization surface. The operator `sessions echo` add paths now reject any target that is not a verified session participant (`targetMatchesSessionParticipant`). Also fixed the native streaming fan-out to use the turn's actual origin (`OriginatingChannel`) instead of stale `last*`, so a webchat/cross-channel trigger renders natively in the pinned channel rather than falling back to the flat post-hoc mirror.
